title: Magic and Sorcery
subtitle: An Act of Disbelief That Destroys the Sorcerer and the Victim

verses.arabic: وَمَا يُعَلِّمَانِ مِنْ أَحَدٍ حَتَّىٰ يَقُولَا إِنَّمَا نَحْنُ فِتْنَةٌ فَلَا تَكْفُرْ ۖ فَيَتَعَلَّمُونَ مِنْهُمَا مَا يُفَرِّقُونَ بِهِ بَيْنَ الْمَرْءِ وَزَوْجِهِ ۚ وَمَا هُم بِضَارِّينَ بِهِ مِنْ أَحَدٍ إِلَّا بِإِذْنِ اللَّهِ ۚ وَيَتَعَلَّمُونَ مَا يَضُرُّهُمْ وَلَا يَنفَعُهُمْ ۚ وَلَقَدْ عَلِمُوا لَمَنِ اشْتَرَاهُ مَا لَهُ فِي الْآخِرَةِ مِنْ خَلَاقٍ
verses.translation: And they [the two angels] do not teach anyone unless they say, 'We are a trial, so do not disbelieve [by practicing magic].' And [yet] they learn from them that by which they cause separation between a man and his wife. But they do not harm anyone through it except by permission of Allah. And the people learn what harms them and does not benefit them. But they certainly knew that whoever purchased the magic would not have in the Hereafter any share.
And the magician will not succeed wherever he goes.

verses.arabic: قَالَ مُوسَىٰ أَتَقُولُونَ لِلْحَقِّ لَمَّا جَاءَكُمْ ۖ أَسِحْرٌ هَٰذَا وَلَا يُفْلِحُ السَّاحِرُونَ
verses.translation: Moses said, 'Do you say [this] about the truth when it has come to you? Is this magic? But magicians will not succeed.'

brief_explanation: The Quran explicitly classifies learning and practicing magic as an act of kufr (disbelief), making it one of the most severe major sins. Allah revealed that the angels Harut and Marut warned people not to practice magic lest they disbelieve — yet people learned it anyway, knowing full well it would cost them their share in the Hereafter. Magic is used to harm, to separate spouses, and to corrupt society — all while the sorcerer earns no benefit but only loss in both worlds. The Quran categorically declares that the sorcerer will never succeed, whether in this life or the next.
capital_punishment: The majority of scholars — including Imam Malik, Ahmad ibn Hanbal, and a view within the Shafi'i school — hold that a Muslim who practices sihr and thereby commits kufr or causes harm (such as murder through magic) is to be executed without being asked to repent, since the nature of the act constitutes disbelief. The Hanafi school also rules execution for the practicing sorcerer. The Shafi'i madhab in its predominant opinion requires establishing the specific crime through evidence. All schools agree it is a capital offense when proven.
